1: # Implementation Plan - Admin Panel for Website Products & Live Previews
2: 
3: This plan outlines the steps to build a complete product and page settings manager inside the admin panel (`App_admin`), with high-fidelity live mobile previews that mimic the React Native client screens.
4: 
5: ---
6: 
7: ## Proposed Changes
8: 
9: ### [Component 1] Admin Routing & Navigation
10: 
11: #### [MODIFY] [App.jsx](file:///Users/sahilpatel/Desktop/New_Mantra_puja.nosync/APP/App_admin/src/App.jsx)
12: - Import `WebsiteProductsManagerPage` from `./WebsiteProductsManagerPage`.
13: - Add a sidebar navigation item: `"Website Products"` pointing to `/website-products`.
14: - Register the route: `<Route path="/website-products" element={<WebsiteProductsManagerPage />} />`.
15: 
16: ---
17: 
18: ### [Component 2] Website Products Manager Screen
19: 
20: #### [NEW] [WebsiteProductsManagerPage.jsx](file:///Users/sahilpatel/Desktop/New_Mantra_puja.nosync/APP/App_admin/src/WebsiteProductsManagerPage.jsx)
21: Create a new admin manager file with:
22: - **Product Management Actions**: Add new product, edit existing product, delete product, and publish/unpublish toggles.
23: - **Form Fields (Mapped to `website_pooja_products` Table)**:
24:   - Basic: Title, Sanskrit Name, Short Name, Slug, Category, Original Price, Offer Price, Duration, Temple Association.
25:   - Media: Main Image URL, Banner Image URL, Gallery Images (JSON list of `{ url, alt }` supporting multiple R2 uploads).
26:   - Details: Tagline (Short description), Description, Spiritual Si
<truncated 596 bytes>
r the store grid page: Banner Image URL, Logo Text Badge (e.g. `'ALL'`), Logo Title (e.g. `'pujas'`), and Subheading.
33:   - Saved to `website_settings` under the key `'view_all_settings'`.
34: - **Live Device Preview Simulator**:
35:   - Displays a virtual mobile device mockup on the right side.
36:   - Allows the user to toggle between two preview modes:
37:     1. **View All Store Grid Preview**: Mimics the mobile `all_pujas.tsx` grid view, showing the customized banner, logo headers, and the grid card layout.
38:     2. **Product Detail Preview**: Mimics the mobile `puja_detail.tsx` page, featuring the sliding image carousel, Select Package cards, combined pricing selector, and accordion dropdown tabs.
39: 
40: ---
41: 
42: ### [Component 3] Expo Client Integration
43: 
44: #### [MODIFY] [all_pujas.tsx](file:///Users/sahilpatel/Desktop/New_Mantra_puja.nosync/APP/mantrapuja/app/all_pujas.tsx)
45: - Add database loading to fetch the view all settings from `website_settings` where `key = 'view_all_settings'`.
46: - Replace the hardcoded store banner image, title badge, logo title, and subheading in the screen's JSX with these dynamically loaded settings.
47: - Implement appropriate fallback values to ensure the screen displays correctly if the database settings are not yet saved.
48: 
49: ---
50: 
51: ## Verification Plan
52: 
53: ### Manual Verification
54: 1. Open the Admin Panel (`App_admin` running at localhost).
55: 2. Go to the new **"Website Products"** tab.
56: 3. Verify that we can create a product (e.g. test multiple gallery images upload and category selection).
57: 4. Review the mobile preview simulator on the right: switch between "Store View" and "Detail Page" preview and verify that it matches the mobile client styling.
58: 5. Edit the View All Banner/Headers in the admin panel and hit save.
59: 6. Open the Expo mobile app, go to the Shop view all page, and confirm the new banner and headings load dynamically!
60:
